민수기 17:2.content: “Speak to the children of Israel, and take rods from them, one for each fathers’ house, of all their princes according to their fathers’ houses, twelve rods. Write each man’s name on his rod.

민수기 17:3.content: You shall write Aaron’s name on Levi’s rod. There shall be one rod for each head of their fathers’ houses.

민수기 17:4.content: You shall lay them up in the Tent of Meeting before the covenant, where I meet with you.

민수기 17:5.content: It shall happen that the rod of the man whom I shall choose shall bud. I will make the murmurings of the children of Israel, which they murmur against you, cease from me.”

민수기 17:6.content: Moses spoke to the children of Israel; and all their princes gave him rods, for each prince one, according to their fathers’ houses, a total of twelve rods. Aaron’s rod was among their rods.

민수기 17:7.content: Moses laid up the rods before Yahweh in the Tent of the Testimony.

민수기 17:8.content: On the next day, Moses went into the Tent of the Testimony; and behold, Aaron’s rod for the house of Levi had sprouted, budded, produced blossoms, and bore ripe almonds.

민수기 17:9.content: Moses brought out all the rods from before Yahweh to all the children of Israel. They looked, and each man took his rod.

민수기 17:10.content: Yahweh said to Moses, “Put back the rod of Aaron before the covenant, to be kept for a token against the children of rebellion; that you may make an end of their complaining against me, that they not die.”

민수기 17:11.content: Moses did so. As Yahweh commanded him, so he did.

민수기 17:12.content: The children of Israel spoke to Moses, saying, “Behold, we perish! We are undone! We are all undone!

민수기 17:13.content: Everyone who keeps approaching Yahweh’s tabernacle, dies! Will we all perish?”
